pages.raw_text:
Corporation Counsel Geraghty
introduced into the city council last
night an initiative and referendum
amendment to the city charter to
be voted upon at the next municipal
election. In speaking of the amend
ment today Mr.. Geraghty said he
wanted 1t understood that he has
been an advocate of the initiative
and referendum law for years and
that he did not expect any opposi-
ton to the proposed measure on the
part Of the members of the city
council.


The amendment provides that Is
per cent Of the number Of votes
cast at the preceding election for
mayor will, when attached to Pc
tition to the city council requesting
the submission of an ordinance to a
vote of the people, make it manda-
tory for the council to do so. The
council shall be required to act
on the petition within 80 days. The
council can act by either passing
the ordinance initiated by popular
demand within the time limit or
by calling a special election at
which said ordinance without alter
alton shall be submitted to vote
of the people. No special election,
however can he held within six
months prior to general municipal
election.


In case the council should pass
the ordinance without it being
voted on by the people, the mayor
vetoed it and the council did not
pass it over his veto, the ordinance
could then be submitted to a vote of
the people.
